title: OVERDRIVE merch-brand logo finishing: variant set, transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, palette + font kit, usage board
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief (cand_42, db_i 2618): brand name "OVERDRIVE" in all caps, scholarship-focused merch brand, "bold, modern, youthful, collegiate, merch-ready — more premium streetwear than charity/nonprofit," a clean scalable graduation-cap element, must work on social PFPs / hoodies / hats / website headers / stickers / watermarks, and the requested deliverable list: primary logo, stacked logo, icon/avatar version, black version, white version, transparent PNGs, SVG, AI/EPS vector source, PDF, color palette, font recommendations, usage guide. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the brief as written ("I need a finalized logo… design") implies authoring a mark from scratch, which is in the capability sheet's [X]/"no" bucket (text-to-image, bespoke type drawing, illustration-from-scratch are NOT available). So this benchmark is honestly scoped to the PRODUCTION / FINISHING half a freelancer actually runs in Adobe once a logo concept exists: the client supplies a rendered raster logo lockup (and a hoodie mock photo for the usage board), and the connector chain removes backgrounds, derives the icon/avatar + social PFP crops, generates the black/white/mono solid-fill variants, vectorizes to a clean SVG source, extracts a palette reference, recommends fonts, and assembles a usage moodboard — then packages the kit. NOT enriched into an unrelated deliverable: every output maps to a line item the client literally asked for. The originality/commercial-rights written confirmation the brief requests is a human/contractual statement, noted in the deliverable spec but not a connector step.
one_line_ask: Take the client's supplied OVERDRIVE raster logo lockup and productionize it into the full merch brand-kit deliverable set — transparent primary/stacked/icon/avatar PNGs, social-PFP crop, black/white/mono variants, a clean SVG vector source, an extracted palette reference, font recommendations, and a usage moodboard — chaining every step in Adobe.
full_brief: OVERDRIVE is a new scholarship-focused merchandise brand (education support via merch sales + scholarship-style campaigns); the look must read bold, modern, youthful, collegiate and merch-ready — premium streetwear, not charity/nonprofit — with the name shown as OVERDRIVE in all caps and a clean, scalable graduation-cap element (no government/university seal, lottery, or generic-education-clipart vibe). The client has approved a rendered raster logo lockup (primary horizontal version on a flat background) and supplies a hoodie mock photo for usage context. Your job, executed entirely with the Adobe connector, is to finish that concept into the production brand kit the client requested: (1) upload the supplied logo lockup into Adobe storage; (2) strip its background to a clean transparent primary logo PNG; (3) derive a tight-cropped icon/avatar from the cap mark and a square social-profile-picture crop sized for PFPs; (4) generate the merch-critical single-color variants — a full BLACK version and a full WHITE version (for dark/light garments, stickers, and watermarks) using subject selection + solid fills, plus one monochromatic brand-tint alt; (5) vectorize the transparent logo into a clean, scalable SVG source for hoodies/hats/large print; (6) produce a flat palette/swatch reference for the color-palette deliverable; (7) recommend brand fonts that match the collegiate-streetwear tone; and (8) assemble the finished variants into a Firefly usage moodboard the client can review, then package everything as the delivered kit. Hard exclusions per Adobe capability ground truth: no from-scratch logo generation, no AI object removal, no compositing, no upscaling — the supplied lockup is the source of truth and every deliverable is derived from it by chaining. The brief's requested AI/EPS source and multipage PDF, and the written originality/commercial-rights confirmation, are noted as human/format-export finishing items layered on top of the connector-produced SVG + PNG masters.

inputs.name: overdrive_logo_lockup.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat 2D vector-style brand logo lockup for a streetwear scholarship merch brand, the wordmark 'OVERDRIVE' in heavy bold all-caps condensed collegiate-athletic sans-serif, with a single clean minimal graduation cap (mortarboard with a short tassel) integrated above or beside the wordmark. Bold modern youthful premium-streetwear feel, NOT a government or university seal, NOT a lottery logo, NOT generic education clipart. Two-color: deep navy and a bright varsity gold. Rendered as a finished horizontal lockup centered on a plain flat light-gray studio background with even margins, crisp anti-aliased edges, no photographic texture, no drop shadow. High resolution, 2400x1400px.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a delivered raster logo export (flat, even, gallery-style on a plain solid background) so background removal and vectorization have clean edges to work on. Text 'OVERDRIVE' must be legible and correctly spelled in all caps — this is why gemini-3-pro-image (text/logo-faithful) is used rather than a photoreal model. Keep it strictly two-color with hard edges; avoid gradients, glow, or 3D bevels that would muddy the vectorize step.

inputs.name: overdrive_hoodie_mock.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid documentary product photo of a folded heather-charcoal cotton hoodie lying flat on a light wooden table in soft natural window light, blank chest area facing camera, realistic fabric weave and ribbing, subtle wrinkles, real cotton texture, no logo printed on it, slight shadow, shot like a real merch flat-lay from a phone, 35mm look, 2000x2000px.
inputs.realism_notes: Used only as a contextual usage tile in the Firefly moodboard (no compositing of the logo onto it — the connector cannot composite). Should look like a genuine candid merch flat-lay (real fabric pores, wrinkles, natural light), not a glossy CGI render. Blank chest so a reviewer can imagine the mark placed there.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_primary_logo_transparent.png
outputs.spec: Primary horizontal lockup, background removed, RGBA transparent, full source resolution (~2400x1400).

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_icon_avatar_transparent.png
outputs.spec: Compact icon/avatar version of the mark: the transparent primary lockup tight-cropped to its content bounds (removes the surrounding empty margin so the whole wordmark+cap lockup sits edge-to-edge as a dense badge). Transparent RGBA. NOTE: this is a tight-cropped full lockup, not a cap-only cutout — a headless content-bounds crop cannot isolate the graduation cap alone from a horizontal wordmark lockup.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_social_pfp_1080.png
outputs.spec: Square social profile-picture asset derived from the transparent icon via image_crop_and_resize to 1080x1080, transparent RGBA. The icon is fit centered into the square frame with transparent margins on the short axis (honest reframe/letterbox of an already-transparent asset — no background is generated or filled and no source canvas is extended).

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_black.png
outputs.spec: Full BLACK (solid #000000) single-color version of the lockup on transparency — for light garments, stickers, watermarks.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_white.png
outputs.spec: Full WHITE (solid #FFFFFF) single-color version of the lockup on transparency — for dark garments and headers.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_mono_navy.png
outputs.spec: Monochromatic navy-tinted single-color brand alt of the lockup.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_source.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG source of the transparent primary logo — scalable master for hoodies/hats/large print (stands in for the AI/EPS source deliverable).

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_palette_swatches.png
outputs.spec: Flat two-tone color-palette reference built honestly from real regions of the cropped icon canvas: the transparent background field is solid-filled with deep brand navy and the mark's own pixels are solid-filled with varsity gold, yielding a navy+gold color-field reference tile for the color-palette deliverable (the accompanying font/spec kit documents exact hex values). NOTE: the fills recolor regions that actually exist on the canvas (background vs. subject) — no fabricated pre-existing swatch blocks are selected.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_font_recommendations.json
outputs.spec: Recommended brand font set (collegiate-streetwear display + supporting text) with names/rationale.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_usage_moodboard_url
outputs.spec: Firefly Boards deep-link assembling all produced variants + the hoodie usage tile for client review.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_brand_kit.zip
outputs.spec: Packaged delivery bundle of all PNG/SVG/JSON masters above (the multipage PDF + AI/EPS export + originality/commercial-rights letter are human finishing items layered on this kit).

connector_workflow.note: Begin upload of the client's supplied raster logo lockup into Adobe storage; returns an upload target/session.
Finalize the logo upload; yields the stored logo asset URN/URL used as the source of truth for the whole chain.
Begin upload of the supplied hoodie mock photo so it has a referenceable Adobe asset URN for the usage board tile.
Finalize the hoodie upload; yields the stored hoodie asset URN consumed later by the Firefly board as the contextual usage tile.
Strip the flat background -> clean transparent PRIMARY logo PNG. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_primary_logo_transparent.png.

Tight-crop the transparent logo to its content bounds -> compact icon/avatar version (the whole wordmark+cap lockup with the empty margin trimmed off, edge-to-edge). This is a content-bounds crop of the FULL lockup, not a cap-only isolate — a headless bounds crop cannot separate just the graduation cap from a horizontal wordmark lockup. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_icon_avatar_transparent.png.

Reframe/resize the transparent icon to a 1080x1080 square social profile-picture asset, fitting the mark centered with transparent margins on the short axis (honest letterbox of an already-transparent asset — no background fill, no canvas extension). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_social_pfp_1080.png.
Select the logo marks (wordmark + cap) on the transparent primary to drive single-color fills.
Solid-fill the selected marks with #000000 -> full BLACK version (solid color only, not generative). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_black.png.
Re-fill the same selected marks with #FFFFFF -> full WHITE version on transparency. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_white.png.
Apply a single-hue navy monochromatic tint -> brand mono alt. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_mono_navy.png.
Vectorize the clean transparent primary into a scalable SVG source master (hoodies/hats/large print). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_source.svg.

Palette build step 1: select the mark (the actual wordmark+cap pixels) on the cropped icon canvas. This selects a region that genuinely exists on the canvas — subject vs. transparent background — rather than fabricating swatch blocks.
Palette build step 2: invert the subject selection to get the surrounding (background) region as a mask, so the two real regions of the canvas can each take one solid brand color.
Palette build step 3: solid-fill the background region with deep brand navy. image_fill_area lays exactly ONE solid color into ONE selected region (solid fill only, not generative).

Palette build step 4: solid-fill the mark region (the reused subject mask from step 13) with varsity gold, yielding the flat navy+gold two-tone color-field reference. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_palette_swatches.png.
Recommend collegiate-streetwear display + supporting text fonts matched to the lockup. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_font_recommendations.json.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: primary_logo_transparent, icon_avatar_transparent, logo_black, logo_white, logo_mono_navy, palette_swatches, hoodie_asset_urn
connector_workflow.note: Assemble all produced variant URNs + the uploaded hoodie usage tile (hoodie_asset_urn from steps 3-4) into a Firefly Boards usage moodboard deep-link for client review. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_usage_moodboard_url.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: primary_logo_transparent, icon_avatar_transparent, social_pfp_1080, logo_black, logo_white, logo_mono_navy, logo_source_svg, palette_swatches, font_recommendations
connector_workflow.note: Single unavoidable local step: zip all connector-produced PNG/SVG/JSON masters into the delivery bundle. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_brand_kit.zip.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1->2 upload the client logo -> logo_asset_urn; steps 3->4 upload the supplied hoodie mock -> hoodie_asset_urn (so the board tile has a referenceable Adobe asset URN). Step 5 (remove_background) consumes logo_asset_urn -> primary_logo_transparent, the hub master. Step 6 content-bounds crops it -> icon_avatar_transparent (a tight-cropped FULL lockup used as the compact icon/avatar, not a cap-only isolate — a bounds crop cannot separate just the cap from a horizontal wordmark lockup); step 7 resizes that icon -> social_pfp_1080. Step 8 selects the marks on primary_logo_transparent -> logo_mark_mask; step 9 fills that mask black (consuming primary_logo_transparent + the mask) -> logo_black; step 10 re-fills the SAME mask on logo_black white -> logo_white. Step 11 tints primary_logo_transparent -> logo_mono_navy. Step 12 vectorizes primary_logo_transparent -> logo_source_svg. Steps 13-16 build the palette swatch reference by recoloring the two real regions of the cropped icon canvas: step 13 selects the subject (the actual mark pixels) -> palette_subject_mask; step 14 inverts that to the background region -> palette_background_mask; step 15 solid-fills the background navy -> palette_build_navy; step 16 solid-fills the mark region (reusing palette_subject_mask) gold -> palette_swatches; each image_fill_area lays exactly ONE solid color into ONE genuinely-present region (no fabricated swatch blocks are prompt-selected). Step 17 recommends fonts from primary_logo_transparent. Step 18 (Firefly board) consumes six prior produced variant/palette outputs + hoodie_asset_urn. Step 19 zips nine prior outputs. Every post-load step consumes at least one prior step's named output; the final deliverable kit is the union of the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon (19 steps, 12 distinct Adobe tools, well past the ≥10 calls / ≥6 tools bar) and a genuine wide mix across A1 raster (remove_background, select_subject, invert_selection, fill_area×4, monochromatic_tint, crop_to_bounds, crop_and_resize, vectorize), A2 document/typography (font_recommend), and A4 asset/board (init/finalize upload, create_firefly_board). It is a real freelancer production job: derive an entire merch brand-kit deliverable set from one approved logo lockup. Difficulty comes from disciplined masking-and-fill reuse (one subject selection drives two single-color logo variants, and a select+invert pair drives the two-tone palette tile), correct ordering (background must be removed before crop/vectorize/board so edges are clean), and staying strictly inside Adobe ground truth — the brief's tempting from-scratch-logo and AI-removal asks are explicitly out of scope, so the spec is honestly scoped to the productionizable half and notes the AI/EPS+PDF+rights-letter as human finishing items. Exactly one unavoidable local step (zip packaging); all core work is connector-headless [C].

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability used. Every step is a real Adobe connector action: uploads (asset_initialize/finalize_file_upload), image_remove_background (native BG strip, not AI object removal), crop_to_bounds/crop_and_resize (gen-free reframe/crop, no canvas growth or extension — the icon is an honest content-bounds tight-crop of the full lockup, not a fabricated cap isolate and not a padded/enlarged canvas), image_select_subject/image_invert_selection (mask-only, non-generative), image_fill_area (SOLID single-color fill of ONE selected region per call -- logo B/W variants via one reused subject mask, and the palette tile via a select-subject + invert pair recoloring the two REAL regions of the icon canvas, no multi-color single-call over-claim and no fabricated pre-existing swatch blocks), image_apply_monochromatic_tint, image_vectorize (raster->SVG trace, not from-scratch art), font_recommend, create_firefly_board. No text-to-image, no from-scratch logo/art, no outpaint, no canvas extension/padding, no upscale/super-resolution, no compositing, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no mask fed to lens_blur, no preview-into-new-file. The hoodie tile is uploaded before board assembly; all board/zip inputs are declared connector outputs or declared inputs. full_brief, outputs[].spec, and chaining_note contain no banned-capability phrasing.
reverify.notes: Fixed all flagged findings: (1) hoodie upload steps (init+finalize) produce hoodie_asset_urn, referenced by the Firefly board instead of the raw file; (2) the icon/avatar deliverable + step 6 note now honestly describe a content-bounds tight-crop of the FULL wordmark+cap lockup (removed the impossible 'cap-mark isolated from the lockup' claim, since a headless bounds crop cannot separate the cap alone); (3) replaced the contrived prompt-select-fabricated-swatch-blocks palette sub-chain (old steps 13-18) with an honest select-subject -> invert-selection -> two solid fills sequence that recolors the two genuinely-present regions (mark vs. background) of the icon canvas into a navy+gold color-field reference. Structured counts reconciled: tool_call_count=19, distinct_adobe_tools=12, tools_used swaps image_select_by_prompt out for image_invert_selection; difficulty_rationale stale '14 steps, 11 tools' corrected to 19 steps / 12 tools; chaining_note renumbered and rewritten to match. Task stays real, coherent, and fully Adobe-connector-doable.
